Apus

Apus is an AI-native iOS development loop for coding agents.
It gives LLM-based tools runtime visibility into iOS apps through the Apus runtime and CLI.

Primary website:
https://getapus.dev/

Primary install command:
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ivanhoe/apus_cli/main/scripts/install.sh | bash

Primary repositories:
- Landing page: https://github.com/ivanhoe/apus_landingpage
- CLI: https://github.com/ivanhoe/apus_cli
- Runtime: https://github.com/ivanhoe/apus

Core concepts:
- The Apus runtime is embedded in an iOS app during development.
- The Apus CLI scaffolds or injects the runtime into Xcode projects.
- The runtime exposes an MCP endpoint inside the running app.
- Coding agents can inspect logs, screenshots, UI hierarchy, network history, and trigger hot reload.

Key commands:
- apus new MyApp
- apus init
- apus doctor --path /path/to/project

MCP endpoint:
- Default local endpoint: http://localhost:9847/mcp

Recommended audience:
- Developers using coding agents to build and debug iOS apps
- Teams exploring MCP-based native app workflows

Canonical docs sources:
- Website: https://getapus.dev/
- CLI repo README: https://github.com/ivanhoe/apus_cli/blob/main/README.md
- Runtime repo README: https://github.com/ivanhoe/apus/blob/main/README.md
